title: Auto-Reset on Rejection

story.i_want_to: have stories automatically reset to pending when I reject them
story.so_that: rejected work is immediately available for re-assignment without manual intervention, creating a self-correcting feedback loop

rationale: Auto-reset is the mechanism that closes the two-tier trust loop. When an orchestrator rejects a story, the agent_status automatically resets to 'pending' and the assigned_agent_id is cleared, making the story immediately available in the 'ready' queue for a new (or the same) agent to claim. This is configurable per tenant (auto_reset_on_rejection setting) because some teams may want manual re-assignment. A webhook event is emitted so external systems can react to auto-resets.

acceptance_criteria.description: When POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject succeeds and tenant setting auto_reset_on_rejection is true (default), the story's agent_status is automatically reset to 'pending', assigned_agent_id is cleared, and assigned_at is cleared.

When auto_reset_on_rejection is false in tenant settings, the reject endpoint sets verified_status to 'rejected' but does NOT reset agent_status. The story remains in its current agent_status (e.g., reported_done) with the agent still assigned.

The auto-reset creates a separate audit_log entry with action='auto_reset', old_state containing the previous agent_status and assigned_agent_id, new_state containing agent_status='pending' and assigned_agent_id=nil, and actor_type='system'.
The auto-reset emits a 'story.auto_reset' webhook event containing story_id, the rejection reason, the previous agent_status, and the previous assigned_agent_id.
The rejection itself emits a 'story.rejected' webhook event containing story_id, orchestrator_id, reason, and findings. This fires before the auto_reset event.
The entire rejection + auto-reset is atomic: if the auto-reset fails, the rejection is also rolled back.
After auto-reset, the story appears in the GET /stories/ready endpoint results (assuming all dependencies are still verified).
The auto-reset also clears reported_done_at timestamp, since the story is no longer in reported_done state.

test_cases.name: Rejection triggers auto-reset when tenant setting is true
preconditions: fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, agent_status: :reported_done, assigned_agent_id: impl_agent.id, reported_done_at: ~U[2026-03-25 10:00:00Z]})
expected_results: Story verified_status is 'rejected', rejected_at is set, rejection_reason is 'Missing test coverage'
Story agent_status is 'pending', assigned_agent_id is nil, assigned_at is nil, reported_done_at is nil
Two audit_log entries: one for 'rejected' action, one for 'auto_reset' action with actor_type='system'

test_cases.name: Rejection does NOT auto-reset when tenant setting is false
expected_results: Story agent_status remains 'reported_done' (NOT reset to pending)
Story assigned_agent_id remains set to impl_agent.id
Only one audit_log entry: 'rejected' action (no 'auto_reset' entry)

test_cases.name: Auto-reset emits story.auto_reset webhook event
steps: Query webhook_events table for this story
expected_results: Two webhook_events created: one with event_type='story.rejected', one with event_type='story.auto_reset'
story.auto_reset payload contains story_id, reason, previous agent_status, previous assigned_agent_id
story.rejected event has earlier inserted_at than story.auto_reset event

test_cases.name: Auto-reset story appears in ready queue
expected_results: The rejected story appears in the ready stories list
Story has agent_status='pending' and is available for claiming

test_cases.name: Atomicity: auto-reset failure rolls back rejection
preconditions: Mock the auto-reset step in Ecto.Multi to return an error
steps: Call Loopctl.Progress.reject_story/3 with valid params but mocked auto-reset failure
expected_results: No audit_log entries created (all rolled back)

test_cases.name: Default tenant settings enable auto-reset
preconditions: fixture(:tenant, %{name: "Test Tenant", settings: %{}}) — empty settings, rely on defaults
expected_results: Returns true (the default value when not explicitly configured)

technical_notes: Integrate auto-reset into the reject_story/3 function in Loopctl.Progress as an additional Ecto.Multi step, conditioned on the tenant's auto_reset_on_rejection setting.
Use Loopctl.Tenants.get_setting/2 with fallback chain: tenant.settings[key] -> default. Default for auto_reset_on_rejection is true.
Webhook events should be enqueued (not delivered inline) via Oban within the same transaction. Use Oban.insert/2 within Ecto.Multi.
The auto_reset audit_log entry must have actor_type='system' to distinguish it from agent-initiated unclaims.
Ensure reported_done_at is cleared alongside assigned_agent_id and assigned_at during auto-reset.
This story modifies the reject path implemented in US-7.2 — it adds the conditional auto-reset step to the existing Ecto.Multi chain.
